How many ways can this be done? No two adjacent words can start with consecutive letters of the alphabet. In your entire response, refrain from the use of ! (i.e. exclamation marks) as punctuation and in general. There should be 8 paragraphs. Paragraphs are separated with the markdown divider: ***

The problem asks for ways to fill five spots with digits from a specific set. We need to find how many combinations make the number divisible by twelve. The set of allowed digits includes zero, two, four, seven, eight, and nine. Each asterisk represents one position in the eleven-digit sequence.

***

Divisibility by twelve requires the number to be divisible by both three and four. This means the sum of all digits must be a multiple of three. The last two digits must form a number divisible by four. These two conditions work together to limit the possible choices.

***

The sum of the fixed digits two, zero, one, six, zero, and two equals eleven. We add the sum of the five chosen digits to this total. The result must be congruent to zero modulo three. This creates a constraint on the sum of the five variable digits.

***

The last digit must be even for the number to be divisible by four. The second to last digit combined with the last digit forms a two-digit number. This pair must be divisible by four. The allowed digits are zero, two, four, seven, eight, and nine.

***

Valid pairs for the last two positions include zero zero, zero four, zero eight, two zero, two four, two eight, four zero, four four, four eight, eight zero, eight four, and eight eight. Some of these pairs use digits not in the allowed set. We must filter the list to ensure every digit is from the set.

***
